display.purpose: The queue number — the hero on every surface. Container-scaled tabular figures.

headline.purpose: 'Now Serving' column title only. The one big, bold heading, in Muji Red Deep.

label.purpose: Uppercase eyebrow labels — station role, queue-group titles. Short only.

shadows.name: ambient
shadows.purpose: The floating offline-toast lift only. The single shadow in the system; never on chips, tiles, or cards at rest.

motion.name: ease-out-quint
motion.purpose: Entrances and layout reflow — tiles springing in, numbers moving between columns.

motion.name: ease-standard
motion.purpose: Default easing for state transitions — tab underline, hover shifts, connection pulse.

motion.name: dur-fast
motion.purpose: Hover/active feedback on chips and buttons.

motion.name: dur-base
motion.purpose: Mode-tab switch and most state transitions.

components.name: Queue Chip
components.description: The resting queue-number token in the staff side panel.
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 3.2rem; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Helvetica, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 500; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: oklch(26% 0.006 40); background: oklch(96.5% 0.005 40); border: 1px solid oklch(90% 0.006 40); border-radius: 8px; }

components.name: Ready Chip
components.description: Served order — Muji Red on a red-tint wash. Pickup variant is tappable.
components.css: .ds-chip-ready { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; min-width: 3.2rem; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Helvetica, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: oklch(42% 0.132 16); background: color-mix(in oklch, oklch(93.5% 0.033 16) 60%, oklch(98.5% 0.0035 40)); border: 1px solid color-mix(in oklch, oklch(42% 0.132 16) 22%, oklch(90% 0.006 40)); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 140ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 140ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 140ms cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1); } .ds-chip-ready:hover { background: color-mix(in oklch, oklch(93.5% 0.033 16) 90%, oklch(98.5% 0.0035 40)); border-color: oklch(42% 0.132 16); } .ds-chip-ready:active { transform: scale(0.96); }

components.name: Hold Chip
components.description: Provisional 'remainder to collect later' state — the one correct dashed border.
components.css: .ds-chip-hold { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; min-width: 3.2rem; padding: 8px 12px; font-family: Helvetica, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 1.05rem; font-weight: 600; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; letter-spacing: -0.02em; color: oklch(26% 0.006 40); background: oklch(96.5% 0.005 40); border: 1px dashed oklch(62% 0.012 40); border-radius: 8px; }

components.name: Undo Button
components.css: .ds-btn-undo { padding: 8px 16px; font-family: Helvetica, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(26% 0.006 40); background: oklch(96.5% 0.005 40); border: 1px solid oklch(90% 0.006 40); border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 140ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-undo:hover { background: oklch(93.5% 0.007 40); } .ds-btn-undo:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(47% 0.154 16); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-undo:disabled { color: oklch(62% 0.012 40); cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Mode Tab
components.description: Staff input-mode switch — text over a 2px underline, active in Muji Red.
components.css: .ds-tabs { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 8px; } .ds-tab { padding: 12px; font-family: Helvetica, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(47% 0.012 40); background: transparent; border: none; border-bottom: 2px solid oklch(90% 0.006 40); cursor: pointer; transition: color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 220ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-tab[data-active="true"] { color: oklch(47% 0.154 16); border-bottom-color: oklch(42% 0.132 16); } .ds-tab:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid oklch(47% 0.154 16); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Connection Status
components.description: Dot + label. Online = steady ink dot; offline = pulsing red. Never color-only.
components.css: .ds-conn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: Helvetica, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif; font-size: 0.8125rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(47% 0.012 40); } .ds-conn-dot { width: 8px; height: 8px; border-radius: 999px; background: oklch(62% 0.012 40); } .ds-conn[data-status="online"] .ds-conn-dot { background: oklch(26% 0.006 40); } .ds-conn[data-status="offline"] .ds-conn-dot { background: oklch(42% 0.132 16); animation: ds-conn-pulse 1.1s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) infinite; } @keyframes ds-conn-pulse { 0%,100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0.3; } }

narrative.overview: muji-qms treats running a queue as a calm ritual, not a control panel. Ink sits on paper-white, a single red mark carries every bit of urgency the room needs, and an oversized queue number is the one thing the eye lands on. The interface recedes so the order is all you see. This is Muji-house restraint applied to a working counter, software that trusts the user to be competent and refuses to shout. Density flexes by surface but the voice never does: the wall display goes airy and huge for distance reading; the staff stations pack a live queue into a side panel without ever feeling like an admin dashboard. One typeface, one accent, a tight warm-neutral ramp, and everything else is space.
keyCharacteristics: Numbers are the interface — tabular figures, generous size, hero weight.
One accent (Muji Red) does all the work; everything else is ink on paper-white.
Hierarchy through scale and position, not decoration.
Quiet confirmation over alarm: a short chime, a brief flash, lowercase copy.
Forgiving under speed: large targets, always-available undo, confirm only where a mistake is costly.

rules.name: The One Mark Rule
rules.body: Muji Red appears on no more than a small fraction of any screen, and only ever to mean live / actionable / now serving. Its rarity is the entire point.

rules.name: The Warm-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Every neutral carries a trace of hue 40 (chroma 0.0035-0.012). Pure-gray neutrals are forbidden; they read cold and clinical against the red.

rules.name: The Legible-Faint Rule
rules.body: Ink Faint is for non-text marks (the connection dot) and disabled controls only. Any text a user is meant to read, including a count or an empty-state message, is Ink Soft or darker. Light gray 'for elegance' fails AA.

rules.name: The Wide-Gamut Mark Rule
rules.body: Brightness comes from lightness; emphasis comes from chroma. Make the red stronger by raising chroma into Display-P3 under @media (color-gamut: p3) and holding lightness fixed; never crank lightness, which desaturates the red and breaks white-on-red button contrast. The TV earns the vivid red; sRGB stations get the gamut-mapped equivalent.

rules.name: The Tabular Number Rule
rules.body: Every queue number, count, and clock uses tabular-nums with -0.02em tracking. Numbers must not reflow or jitter as digits change.

rules.name: The Single-Family Rule
rules.body: One typeface, full stop. Hierarchy is built from weight (400/500/600/700) and scale. A second family is forbidden.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest and separated by tone and 1px lines. A shadow appears only when an element genuinely overlays the page (the offline toast).

dos: Do make the queue number the largest, heaviest thing on every surface. Tabular figures, -0.02em tracking, sized by container query on the board.
Do reserve Muji Red (oklch(42% 0.132 16)) for live / actionable / now-serving only, on a small fraction of the screen.
Do keep neutrals warm (hue 40, chroma 0.0035-0.012). Separate surfaces by tone plus 1px Line borders.
Do stay flat at rest; the only shadow is the offline toast's ambient lift.
Do confirm softly — a 2-note chime, a brief flash, lowercase status copy.
Do keep tap targets large and undo always reachable; gate only costly actions behind a dialog.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion; every entrance needs a still fallback.
Do push the wall board toward AAA contrast and maximum number scale; it's read across a room.
donts: Don't build a loud fast-food menu board: no high-saturation color fields, no flashing, no animated-everything QSR signage.
Don't build a generic SaaS dashboard: no cards-everywhere reflex, no decorative blue, no widgets that ignore the physical room.
Don't go playful or gamified: no mascots, badges, emoji, bouncy/elastic motion, or rounded-cartoon styling.
Don't let Muji Red drift pink or coral, and never use it decoratively. If red isn't saying 'live', it's wrong.
Don't introduce a second typeface or a display face. One Helvetica, weight contrast only.
Don't add drop shadows to chips, tiles, or cards. Deepen the tone or the border instead.
Don't use pure-gray (chroma 0) neutrals; they read cold against the red.
Don't let numbers reflow or jitter — tabular figures everywhere a count or clock changes.
Don't use a colored border-left/border-right stripe as an accent; full borders or background tints only.
